name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
description: Top risks for applications built on large language models. Applies both to user architectures containing AI workloads and to this application itself (see docs/ai-governance/). Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Prompt Injection
controls.summary: Attacker-controlled input (direct or embedded in retrieved content) manipulates the model into ignoring instructions or misusing tools.

check_hints.fail_message: AI workload has no input filtering or prompt-injection defenses.

controls.remediation: Segregate system instructions from user content, treat all retrieved/tool content as untrusted, apply input classifiers and Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shields, and require human confirmation for consequential actions.

controls.title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.summary: The model reveals confidential data from its context, training, or connected systems.
controls.remediation: Minimize sensitive data in prompts, apply output filtering/DLP, scope tool access per user authorization, and log disclosures for review.

controls.title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
controls.summary: Compromised models, datasets, plugins, or packages undermine the application.
controls.remediation: Use vetted model providers (Azure OpenAI), pin dependencies, scan packages/containers in CI, and maintain an AI bill of materials.

controls.title: Data and Model Poisoning
controls.summary: Manipulated training or grounding data induces harmful or biased model behavior.
controls.remediation: Control provenance of fine-tuning/RAG data, validate and version datasets, and monitor output drift with evaluations.

controls.title: Improper Output Handling
controls.summary: Model output is passed to downstream systems (browsers, shells, SQL) without validation, enabling injection attacks.
controls.remediation: Treat model output as untrusted input: encode for the destination context, validate against schemas, and never execute generated code or queries without sandboxing.

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.summary: The model is granted more tools, permissions, or autonomy than the task requires, amplifying the blast radius of manipulation.

check_hints.fail_message: AI workload's tool access is not restricted by an allow-list.

controls.remediation: Allow-list tools per role, scope tool permissions to least privilege, require human approval for destructive actions, and audit every tool invocation.

controls.title: System Prompt Leakage
controls.summary: Secrets or sensitive logic embedded in system prompts are extracted by users.
controls.remediation: Never place credentials or security-critical logic in prompts; enforce controls server-side and assume prompts are readable.

controls.title: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
controls.summary: RAG stores can be poisoned or leak data across tenants through weak access controls on embeddings.
controls.remediation: Apply per-tenant filtering and authorization on vector queries, validate ingested content, and monitor retrieval anomalies.

controls.title: Misinformation
controls.summary: Confident but wrong output (hallucination) leads users to unsafe decisions.
controls.remediation: Ground responses in authoritative sources (framework data via tools), display provenance, run evaluation suites in CI, and label AI output clearly.

controls.title: Unbounded Consumption
controls.summary: Uncontrolled request volume or token usage causes denial of service or runaway cost.

check_hints.fail_message: AI workload has no rate limiting or token budget controls.

controls.remediation: Enforce per-user rate limits and token budgets, set provisioned throughput alerts, and use APIM policies in front of Azure OpenAI.
